quick_facts.label: Recruiting examination
quick_facts.value: The UPSC Civil Services Examination (CSE) is the common recruitment examination for multiple central services listed in its 2026 notice, including the Indian Audit and Accounts Service (IA&AS), Indian Corporate Law Service (ICLS), Indian Civil Accounts Service, Indian Defence Accounts Service, Indian Information Service, Indian Postal Service and others in Group A.

quick_facts.label: Selection and allocation
quick_facts.value: CSE has a Preliminary screening stage followed by the Main written examination and Interview/Personality Test. Final ranking is based on Main written and interview marks, and service allocation takes account of rank and the candidate’s stated service preferences.

quick_facts.label: Baseline eligibility context
quick_facts.value: For CSE 2026, UPSC required a graduate degree or equivalent qualification. The general age band was 21 to 32 years as on August 1, 2026, with specified relaxations; eligibility rules and dates are cycle-specific and must be checked in the applicable notice.

quick_facts.label: Illustrative service: IA&AS
quick_facts.value: IA&AS is a Union Group A civil service forming the middle and top management of the Indian Audit and Accounts Department, through which the Comptroller and Auditor General of India exercises its mandate.

quick_facts.label: Illustrative service: ICLS
quick_facts.value: The Ministry of Corporate Affairs describes ICLS as supporting corporate-law enforcement, including incorporation, regulation, investor protection, corporate social responsibility and corporate-governance implementation.

sections.heading: Responsibilities vary by the allocated Group A service
paragraphs: “Allied civil services” is a useful umbrella label rather than one single cadre or job description. A successful CSE candidate is allocated to a particular service, so day-to-day work follows that service’s statutory and administrative mandate.

In IA&AS, officers support the CAG’s constitutional audit and accounts mandate. The official IA&AS description identifies work involving audit of Union and state governments and compilation of state accounts; Accountant General offices may also undertake entitlement functions such as pension and provident-fund work for state-government employees. In ICLS, the Ministry of Corporate Affairs describes the service’s intended role as providing capacity for corporate-law enforcement, incorporation and regulation, investor protection, CSR and corporate governance. These examples illustrate why the work can range from public audit and financial accountability to corporate regulation and compliance.
bullets: IA&AS example: public-sector audit, state-account compilation and related entitlement administration.
ICLS example: corporate-law administration and enforcement, registry-related regulation, investor-protection and governance work.
Applicants should study the mandate, cadre-controlling authority and postings of each individual service before placing CSE preferences.

sections.heading: Primary route: UPSC Civil Services Examination
paragraphs: The principal entry route is the annual UPSC CSE. UPSC’s 2026 notification expressly included IA&AS and ICLS among the Group A services/posts covered by the examination. The examination comprises a Preliminary examination for selection to the Main examination, followed by a Main written examination and an Interview/Personality Test.

For the 2026 cycle, UPSC stated that applicants needed a graduate degree or equivalent qualification. The notice set the general age requirement at 21–32 as on August 1, 2026 and provided category-specific age and attempt relaxations. Those details are not permanent career rules: candidates should rely on the notification and examination rules for the year in which they apply.

Service allocation is not achieved merely by clearing an individual service-specific test. UPSC states that candidates are allotted services based on final rank and the preferences they express for the available services and posts.
bullets: Apply through the UPSC online application process during the relevant CSE application window.
Clear Preliminary examination; its marks are screening marks and do not determine final merit.
Clear the Main written examination and Interview/Personality Test.
Submit service preferences carefully, because rank and preferences both inform allocation.

sections.heading: Capabilities developed and assessed
paragraphs: Because Group A allied services cover specialised public functions, useful capabilities include analytical reading, structured writing, ethical judgment, communication, evidence-based decision-making and the ability to work with legal, financial and administrative records. This is also consistent with the CSE design: the Main examination includes papers in governance, Constitution and polity; economic development; ethics, integrity and aptitude; essays; and an optional subject, while the interview assesses suitability for a public-service career.

For service-specific effectiveness, candidates should build domain literacy after allocation. For example, IA&AS work calls for audit and accounts understanding, while ICLS work benefits from corporate-law, regulatory and compliance understanding. A particular degree in these fields is not stated as the general CSE minimum qualification; UPSC’s baseline requirement is a graduate degree or equivalent.
bullets: Clear, concise written analysis and communication.
Public-service ethics, integrity and sound judgment.
Ability to interpret rules, records, data and evidence.
Service-specific learning in areas such as public audit/accounts or corporate law/regulation after allocation.

sections.heading: Workplaces and postings
paragraphs: Work location depends on the allocated service and its organisation. IA&AS has a national operating footprint: official CAG material describes state Accountant General offices, regionally located Principal Director (Audit) offices for central-government audit, and international audit offices in Kuala Lumpur, London and Washington, DC. This means postings can involve headquarters, field-office and state-level audit/accounts environments.

For ICLS, Ministry of Corporate Affairs material links the service to the corporate regulatory system, including Registrars of Companies and Official Liquidators, and identifies the Indian Institute of Corporate Affairs as the induction-level and in-service training academy for ICLS. Accordingly, its work is connected to ministry, regulatory and registry-facing settings rather than a single standard office location.
bullets: IA&AS: CAG/Indian Audit and Accounts Department offices in states and regions, with some international audit presence.
ICLS: Ministry of Corporate Affairs and corporate-regulatory/registry ecosystem, including Registrar of Companies-related offices.
Posting, travel and field exposure vary substantially by service, assignment and career stage.

sections.heading: Career progression is cadre-specific
paragraphs: Promotion structures are determined by the individual service rather than by the generic “allied civil services” label. IA&AS provides a clear official example: the National Academy of Audit and Accounts states that an officer joins as Deputy Accountant General and may progress through Senior Deputy Accountant General, Accountant General, Principal Accountant General, Additional Deputy CAG and Deputy CAG; equivalent central-audit designations include Principal Director and Director General at relevant levels.

This example should not be treated as the promotion ladder for ICLS or every other Group A CSE service. Candidates should consult the cadre-controlling authority and current service rules for the allocated service, since promotion, deputation and seniority arrangements are service-specific.
bullets: Illustrative IA&AS path: Deputy AG → Senior Deputy AG → AG → Principal AG → Additional Deputy CAG → Deputy CAG.
Central-audit equivalents cited by CAG include Principal Director and Director General.
Career pathways in other allied services differ and should be verified with their respective authorities.
